name: Smith Machine Bench Press
resistance_profile: The fixed vertical bar path removes side-to-side balance demand, letting you press heavy and near failure safely; tension is fairly even with the usual mid-range sticking point.
cues: Line the bar up over the lower chest, not the neck
Control the bar to the chest, press to a smooth lockout
execution_steps: Set an adjustable bench under the Smith bar so the bar lines up over the lower chest.
Set the safety stops just below chest level and unrack by rotating the bar off the hooks.
Pinch the shoulder blades back and down and take a grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Lower the bar under control to the lower chest with a slight tuck of the elbows.
Press the bar back up to a controlled lockout, then re-rack by rotating the hooks at the top.
good_when: Training to failure without a spotter
Beginners learning a stable pressing groove
Adding pressing volume with less stabilization fatigue
bad_when: You want to train free-weight stabilizers and balance
The fixed bar path aggravates your shoulders
You want barbell-bench carryover for powerlifting
common_errors: Setting the bar path too high toward the neck
Bouncing the bar off the chest
Forgetting to set the safety stops
progressions: Pause the bar on the chest each rep
